================================================================================
FORM 44 - ADVERSE DRUG REACTION REPORT - COMPLETE EXAMPLE
================================================================================

PATIENT INFORMATION
-------------------
Patient ID: PAT-2026-001234
Age: 52
Gender: Female
Weight: 65 kg
Date of Birth: 1973-10-15

SUSPECT DRUG INFORMATION
------------------------
Drug Name: Paracetamol
Manufacturer: ABC Pharmaceuticals Ltd
Generic Name: Acetaminophen
Batch Number: BATCH-2026-45782
Expiry Date: 2027-12-31
Date Drug Started: 2026-02-15
Date Drug Stopped: 2026-03-12
Route of Administration: Oral
Dose: 500 mg
Frequency: Twice daily (morning and evening)
Indication: Fever and body ache
Duration of Therapy: 25 days

SUSPECTED ADVERSE REACTION
--------------------------
Reaction Type: Gastrointestinal bleeding
Adverse Reaction: Severe abdominal pain with blood in stools
Date of Onset: 2026-02-28
Onset Date: 2026-02-28
Duration: 4 days
Severity: Severe
Outcome: Hospitalized for 48 hours, required blood transfusion
Outcome Detail: Patient discharged after stabilization on day 3
Was Reaction Serious: Yes
Which Serious Outcome: Hospitalization required

MEDICAL AND DRUG HISTORY
------------------------
Past Medical History: Peptic ulcer disease (history), Hypertension (controlled)
Previous ADR: Allergy to Aspirin (rash)
Concomitant Medications:
  - Warfarin 5 mg daily (anticoagulant)
  - Metoprolol 50 mg twice daily (antihypertensive)
  - Pantoprazole 20 mg daily (PPI)
  
Alcohol Use: Occasional
Smoking: Non-smoker

REPORTER INFORMATION
--------------------
Reporter Name: Dr. Ramesh Kumar Sharma
Title: Medical Director
Reporter Phone: +91-9876543210
Reporter Email: dr.sharma@hospital.com
Report Date: 2026-03-15
Date of Report: 2026-03-15
Other Substance Use: None

ACTION TAKEN WITH SUSPECTED DRUG
---------------------------------
Drug Action: immediately withdrawn
Follow-up Actions Taken: 
  - Blood transfusion (2 units PRBC)
  - Proton pump inhibitor therapy initiated
  - Endoscopy performed (found gastric ulcer with bleeding)
  - Patient advised to avoid NSAIDs and aspirin in future

DECHALLENGE / RECHALLENGE INFORMATION
-------------------------------------
Dechallenge: Yes
Dechallenge Response: Bleeding stopped within 24 hours of drug withdrawal
Rechallenge: No
Reason for No Rechallenge: Risky given patient's medical history and previous reaction

ASSESSMENT OF CAUSALITY
------------------------
Temporal Relationship: Consistent (reaction started 13 days after drug initiation)
Dose Response: Yes (higher dose within therapeutic range)
Alternative Causes: Interaction with Warfarin possible (known potentiator of bleeding risk)
Consistency with Literature: Yes (GI bleeding is known ADR of paracetamol in susceptible patients)
Dechallenge Evidence: Positive (reaction resolved after drug stopped)

CAUSALITY ASSESSMENT: PROBABLE

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION
----------------------
Associated Lab Test Values:
  - Hemoglobin: 7.2 g/dL (normal: 12-16) - LOW
  - Hematocrit: 22% (normal: 36-46%) - LOW
  - INR: 4.2 (therapeutic for Warfarin is 2-3) - ELEVATED

Healthcare Provider Information
Reporter Name: Dr. Rajesh Kumar
Designation: Senior Medical Officer
Hospital/Clinic: City General Hospital
Department: Emergency Medicine
Contact Number: +91-9876543210
Email: rajesh.kumar@cityhospital.com
Reporter Registration Number: REG-2023-4567

Date of Report: 2026-03-27
Report Type: Initial Report
Report Status: Complete

================================================================================
END OF FORM 44
